_notes: VO for record_update_demo.sh. Each segment's `marker` is a unique substring of a title()/say() line in the recording script — record_update_demo.sh greps the rendered .cast for that exact text to find the segment's REAL on-screen timestamp (via extract_vo_timestamps.py), so VO placement never drifts even though command runtimes (network round-trips) vary run to run. Keep markers unique and keep this list in the same order as the script.

segments.marker: PDK update — new since the last demo

segments.text: Freshly installed from PyPI. Version zero point one point eight.
New command — pdk fund. It tops up any account with POT from Alice. No need to know Alice exists.
And send now has a dry run. Preview the fee and the outcome, before you submit anything.
Here is the real reason the TypeScript companion exists.

segments.marker: python3 attempt_assets_signing.py   # sign an Assets pallet call from Python
segments.text: Python tries to sign an Assets pallet call on Portaldot, and fails. Bad signature, at the RPC layer, before it even reaches the chains own dispatch logic.
pdk-ts signs the exact same call. Create an asset. Mint it. Transfer it. All real. All on-chain.
And its not just a claim. Heres the balance, read straight from chain storage. Bob and Charlie, exactly as expected.

segments.marker: Assets pallet signing Python cannot do at all
segments.text: pdk, oh one, point eight. pdk-ts, alpha seven. The one thing Python cannot do, pdk-ts does.
